commands.review_summary:
The PR reuses GitHub comment mutation responses, bounds PR-close coverage comment hydration when comment counts are absent, memoizes covering PR hydration inside a proof check, and adds repair `GH_BIN`/`GH_BIN_ARGS` support with tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR is a performance cleanup rather than a bug report. Source inspection shows current main re-fetches the durable review comment and full-paginates repair coverage comments when counts are absent.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer review plus contributor-provided real behavior proof, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: perf: reduce GitHub comment round trips
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

ClawSweeper has a few GitHub comment/proof paths that do avoidable repeat reads. Durable review comment sync writes a comment and then re-fetches the issue comments list to rediscover the same comment. PR close coverage proof can fall back to full comment pagination when GitHub does not provide a comment count. Repair apply had the same unknown-count full-pagination behavior, and its Windows tests could not reliably force the fake `gh` shim.

## Why This Change Was Made

This reuses GitHub's POST/PATCH comment response from `upsertReviewComment` when the response already identifies the written comment, keeping the existing list lookup as a fallback for malformed or empty responses.

Coverage-proof comment hydration now stays bounded when issue comment counts are absent: the main ClawSweeper path uses the existing link-header context window helper, and repair apply uses a local first/tail link-header window before falling back to full pagination only when GitHub does not expose usable page links. The coverage proof gate also memoizes candidate PR hydration inside one proof operation, while the post-proof freshness recheck remains a fresh GitHub read.

The repair GitHub wrapper now honors `GH_BIN` and `GH_BIN_ARGS`, matching the main command wrapper pattern and allowing Windows tests to route `gh` calls through a JS shim without relying on executable extension resolution.

## User Impact

Apply/comment/proof runs make fewer GitHub API calls and avoid unnecessary full comment pagination on large discussions, without changing close gates, freshness rechecks, durable comment markers, or public review output.

## Evidence

Head proofed: `d0c43dc7c6`.

- Duplicate scan before opening found no exact open PR for `upsertReviewComment`, coverage-proof comment windows, unknown comment counts, or `GH_BIN_ARGS`; broader automation PR hits were unrelated.
- `pnpm run build:all` passed.
- `pnpm run lint` passed.
- `pnpm run format:check` passed.
- `pnpm run check:active-surface` passed.
- `pnpm run check:limits` passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `node --test test/context.test.ts` passed: 19 tests, 19 pass.
- `node --test test/clawsweeper.test.ts` passed: 58 tests, 58 pass.
- `node --test test/repair/apply-result.test.ts` passed: 34 tests, 34 pass.
- `node --test test/repair/github-cli.test.ts` passed: 3 tests, 3 pass.
- `codex review -c 'service_tier="fast"' --uncommitted` reported no actionable correctness issues.

Focused behavior proof from the changed tests:

```text
node --test test/clawsweeper.test.ts
...
# durable review comment sync test asserted only one paginated /issues/321/comments fetch after the write response was reused
pass 58, fail 0

node --test test/repair/apply-result.test.ts
...
repair apply bounds covering PR comments when issue comment count is absent
# asserted no --slurp full pagination, exactly two -i comment page reads, and per_page=100 bounded reads
pass 34, fail 0

node --test test/repair/github-cli.test.ts
...
ghText honors GH_BIN and GH_BIN_ARGS wrappers
pass 3, fail 0
```

Local full `pnpm run check` on this Windows checkout passed `check:active-surface`, `check:limits`, `build:all`, and `lint`, then failed in `test:unit` for pre-existing local/Windows issues: Codex proof subprocesses read a local config with `service_tier = "default"`, one command test observes CRLF output, the exact-review failure test does not hit the expected fake-Codex path, and a POSIX mode assertion is not portable on Windows. A separate `pnpm run test:repair` run also failed on unrelated Windows/toolchain setup cases such as fake `gh.js` direct execution, missing `bun`, and PATH-isolated `pnpm`. The edited surface has the focused passing proof above; GitHub CI should provide the full Linux `pnpm check` signal.
